title: The agent-messaging-service repo carries an oddkit overlay rooted at canon/
content: The agent-messaging-service repository carries its own oddkit overlay rooted at a canon/ tree alongside the long-form spec docs at the repo root. Consumers pass knowledge_base_url=https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service to oddkit tools to make the overlay discoverable. The overlay extends the klappy.dev baseline; cross-program canon (vodka-architecture, definition-of-done, mode-discipline, the writing canon, the AI voice clichés constraint) lives upstream and is referenced via klappy:// URIs, not duplicated. Overlay structure mirrors klappy.dev: canon/decisions, canon/constraints, canon/principles, with a knowledge-base.md root pointer and an ams://docs/operating-contract-pointer for LLM agents working on the repo.

title: Long-form spec docs are not converted to canon; canon extracts their load-bearing constraints
content: The long-form spec docs (SPEC, AMS, PROTOCOL, ARCHITECTURE, POC-INFRA, POC-PLAN, ESSAY, GLOSSARY, HORIZON, PATTERNS, README) are not rewritten as canon articles. They are the long-form reasoning surface and are governed by SPEC.md §14 revision discipline. The canon/ tree extracts their load-bearing decisions and constraints into addressable, rule-shaped articles per klappy://canon/principles/dry-canon-says-it-once. Each canon article points back to the long-form source via the derives_from frontmatter; there is no content duplication.

title: Tier 1 for irreversible decisions; Tier 2 for evolving conventions
content: Tier assignments in the overlay follow upstream norms: Tier 1 for one-way doors and load-bearing irreversible decisions (tokens-not-messages, magic-link-as-URL, per-account stream ownership, two-door registration, dream-house wire and edge wrappers, SPEC-as-locking-surface, HORIZON-as-constraint-set, wire conformance, permanent non-goals); Tier 2 for evolving conventions and patterns (conversation-not-room vocabulary, wrapper-stays-cheap, vodka-architecture-applied, two-agent conversation conventions, own-stream-echo-must-be-filtered, operator-as-subscriber, MCP wrapper conformance for conversational AI). Stability values follow tier: stable for Tier 1, semi_stable for Tier 2 conventions expected to evolve as more implementations land.

title: Convention canon for two-agent conversation is recommended-strength, not prescriptive
content: The new convention canon for the two-conversational-AI-assistants use case is authored as recommendations, not wire requirements. The wire stays vodka per ams://canon/constraints/permanent-non-goals; convention belongs above the wire in metadata-declared form. Implementations may diverge from the recommended convention by declaring divergence in the capabilities metadata key. Two implementations that follow the convention interop without prior coordination; two that diverge interop on whatever subset they share. This preserves the foundation play while still giving v1 implementers a reference point that prevents the most common failure modes (echo loops, endless ping-pong, stalls, simultaneity confusion).

title: Canon URIs use ams:// for AMS-original; klappy:// for upstream
content: Canon URIs in the overlay use the ams:// namespace for AMS-original articles and klappy:// for cross-references to upstream baseline canon. The split makes provenance unambiguous: any URI starting with ams:// is governed by this repo's revision discipline; any URI starting with klappy:// is governed by the klappy.dev baseline. Cross-references in derives_from, complements, governs, and See Also use the appropriate namespace. Relative file paths are not used in cross-references because they break under overlay/baseline merging.

title: Every canon article has a What This Is Not section
content: Every canon article in the overlay includes a "What this is not" section per the upstream canon template's dogma-avoidance rule. The section names what the article does not claim, what it does not foreclose, and what it does not enforce. This prevents the canon from drifting into universal-claim form and preserves the layered-opinion model that AMS's vodka discipline depends on.

title: Voice is checked against ai-voice-cliches before commit
content: Every canon article in the overlay is voice-checked against klappy://canon/constraints/ai-voice-cliches before commit. No negation parallelism tics, no formulaic transitions, no em-dash overuse, no sycophantic openers, no generic positive descriptors. The AMS docs were already written in a clean voice; canon extraction maintains it.

title: The wire's own-stream echo is the most common demo-killer for conversational AI subscribers
content: The PROTOCOL §4.1 commitment that the server pushes own-stream tokens to the emitting client is the single most likely place for a naïve conversational AI implementation to fail. Without an explicit owner_account_id filter, the agent reads its own emissions as input, generates a response to its own response, and loops until rate limits intervene. This is so easy to miss that it warrants its own canon article (ams://canon/principles/own-stream-echo-must-be-filtered) and is also encoded as convention 6 in ams://canon/constraints/two-agent-conversation-conventions. Defense in depth: the wrapper filters by default, and the agent filters again.

title: Comprehensive convention canon prevents the demo gate from devolving into per-implementation reinvention
content: The SPEC §3.2 demo gate scenario (two Claude Code instances exchanging tokens through one AMS conversation, no human in the wire) requires application-layer conventions the wire does not own: turn boundaries, end-of-turn signaling, handshake, termination defaults, system-prompt isolation, own-stream echo handling, capabilities schema. Without canon for these, every demo implementation reinvents them and inconsistent reinventions fail to interop. The convention canon provides v1 defaults so two well-behaved implementations can interop without prior coordination, while keeping the wire vodka so divergent conventions remain possible above it.

title: An overlay-internal canon-integration audit is not yet automated
content: The klappy.dev baseline ships a canon-integration audit (klappy://canon/constraints/canon-integration-audit) that checks cross-references and concept consistency before merge. The agent-messaging-service overlay does not currently run such an audit on its own canon files. Open: when the overlay grows beyond the bootstrap set, an audit pass that walks ams:// URIs and validates derives_from/complements/governs/See Also targets becomes necessary. Likely re-entry signal: third or fourth canon addition where a cross-reference is wrong or missing.

title: The four convention canon articles are unproven by a second implementation
content: The convention canon for two-agent conversation (turn sentinels, capabilities schema, termination defaults, operator role declarations) is authored from first-principles reasoning plus the SPEC §3.2 demo gate. It has not yet been tested by a second independent implementation. Re-entry signal: the second wrapper class (likely webhook adapter per SPEC.md §10) lands and forces revision of any convention that did not survive contact with reality. Until then, the convention is recommended-not-required and may be revised by SPEC §14 process if the demo gate forces a change.

title: Operator commits the overlay; PR not opened by this session
content: The overlay file set was authored in this session and produced as a downloadable bundle. The operator commits to the agent-messaging-service repository themselves; this session did not open a PR because credentials were leaked in plain text in the conversation and treated as compromised. After commit, the next session can verify overlay loading by running oddkit_search with knowledge_base_url=https://github.com/klappy/agent-messaging-service and confirming overlay_doc_count > 0.

title: Canon URI convention: ams://canon/{decisions|constraints|principles}/{slug}
content: The URI convention for AMS overlay canon is ams://canon/{section}/{slug} where section is one of decisions (numbered Dxxxx-named-slug), constraints (named slug), or principles (named slug). Documentation pointers use ams://docs/{slug}. The root pointer is ams://knowledge-base. The convention mirrors klappy.dev's canon URI shape with the ams:// namespace replacing klappy://. URIs are content-addressable by the search index once the overlay is loaded; they survive file moves within the convention as long as the slug is preserved.

title: Convention vocabulary: ams.convention.v1 namespace for capability-declared conventions
content: The capabilities metadata key carries an ams.convention.v1 sub-key when the subscriber follows the recommended convention from ams://canon/constraints/two-agent-conversation-conventions. Future revisions of the convention ship as ams.convention.v2, etc., with backward-compatibility advice in the upgrade notes. Implementations that do not follow the convention either omit the sub-key entirely or set it to false; alternative conventions live under their own organization-namespaced sub-keys. This preserves the metadata slot's vodka shape (AMS does not validate, AMS does not schema) while still giving the convention a discoverable name.
